meaning: Maintain the helicopter in a stationary position above a point with zero ground speed.
simpleMeaning: Stay still in the air above one spot.
portugueseMeaning: Manter o helicóptero parado no ar sobre um ponto.
operationalMeaning: Hover is used for helipad ops, sling loads, SAR, and confined-area landings.
whoUsesIt: Helicopter pilots; Tower or Helo control may request hover or report hover complete.
whenUsed: Before landing on a helipad, during external load work, or when ATC requests position hold.
whyUsed: Helicopters can hold position without a runway — hover is core rotorcraft technique.

realExamples.text: PT-ABC, report hover complete over the helipad.
realExamples.speaker: Tower

realExamples.text: Unable to hover due to gusts, request delay.
realExamples.speaker: Pilot

realExamples.text: Hovering over the hospital helipad, PT-ABC.
realExamples.speaker: Pilot

icaoQuestions: When would a helicopter pilot report hover complete to Tower?
What factors make a hover more difficult in hot weather?
commonMistakes: Using 'stop' instead of 'hover' for rotorcraft position reports.
Not reporting when unable to maintain hover in strong gusts.
Confusing hover with slow forward flight near the ground.
pronunciationTips: HOVER COMPLETE — stress COMPLETE on the report.

compareTerms.note: Hold in fixed-wing means orbit or position; hover is zero ground speed.
Orbit is circling; hover is stationary.

didYouKnow: High density altitude reduces hover performance significantly in Brazil's interior.
captainStory: SAR training off the coast, we held a hover while the winchman dropped to the deck — Tower only cleared us to land after 'hover complete.'
